title: I Am Where I Am. There's No Behind.
subtitle: An affirmation for releasing the idea of being behind on your own life
excerpt: A short affirmation for the chronic feeling of being behind on a timeline that nobody actually set. Rejects the premise that life has a schedule you can fall behind on and replaces it with the actual position: here, now, starting from this.

content.text: A short affirmation for the catching-up feeling. The premise of being 'behind' requires a schedule that was agreed on. Most of the time, it wasn't. This affirmation names what is actually true: you are where you are, and that is the real starting point.
Say it in the morning, before the day begins. Three times. Slowly.
I am where I am. There's no behind. This is where I start.
Three sentences. The first names the real position. The second removes the judgement. The third gives the day a starting point. All three together are the counter-statement to the catching-up loop.
